sections.label: Residential
sections.blurb: Coverage that protects your home, belongings, and personal liability.

topics.title: Homeowners insurance
topics.definition: A policy that protects your home structure, your belongings, and your personal liability if someone is injured on your property.
topics.covers: Damage to the home and attached structures, personal property, loss of use while repairs happen, and liability for injuries to others.
topics.doesntCover: Flood and earthquake damage (separate policies), normal wear and tear, and damage from neglected maintenance.
topics.whoNeeds: Anyone who owns a home — and it is usually required by your mortgage lender.

topics.title: Auto insurance
topics.definition: A policy that covers financial loss from accidents, theft, and damage involving your vehicle, plus liability for injury or damage you cause.
topics.covers: Liability for injury and property damage you cause, plus optional collision and comprehensive coverage for your own vehicle.
topics.doesntCover: Routine maintenance, mechanical breakdown, and (without added coverage) a rental while your car is repaired.
topics.whoNeeds: Anyone who owns or regularly drives a vehicle — minimum liability coverage is required in almost every state.

sections.label: Commercial
sections.blurb: Coverage that protects a business, its property, its people, and its liability.

topics.title: Business owner's policy (BOP)
topics.definition: A bundled policy that combines property insurance and general liability into one package, often at a lower cost than buying them separately.
topics.covers: Business property (equipment, inventory, furnishings), general liability, and usually business-interruption income loss.
topics.doesntCover: Employee injuries (workers' compensation), professional mistakes (professional liability), and commercial vehicles.
topics.whoNeeds: Small and mid-sized businesses with a physical location or business property who want bundled, cost-effective coverage.

topics.title: General liability insurance
topics.definition: A policy that protects a business from claims of bodily injury, property damage, and advertising injury caused to others.
topics.covers: Third-party bodily injury and property damage, legal defense costs, and personal and advertising injury claims.
topics.doesntCover: Your own property damage, employee injuries, professional errors, and damage to work you performed.
topics.whoNeeds: Almost every business — it is the baseline liability protection clients and landlords often require.
